questions.questionText: What does PPPoE stand for in WISP router configuration?
Which setting must be entered when configuring a router for PPPoE WAN type?
Where is the WAN port typically located on a home router?
options: On the bottom of the device
Beside the LAN ports and usually colored differently
Directly connected to the Wi-Fi antenna

questions.questionText: Why do WISPs commonly use PPPoE instead of DHCP for WAN connections?
options: PPPoE allows better client authentication and session control
DHCP does not work on wireless networks
PPPoE prevents internet theft through MAC filtering

questions.questionText: What is the typical default IP address used to access a router’s configuration page?
Which feature is commonly used to prevent unauthorized access to the Wi-Fi network?
After router setup, which indicator light confirms internet connectivity on most consumer routers?
In a WISP setup, which device is typically connected directly to the WAN port of the router?
options: POE adapter connected to the outdoor antenna

questions.questionText: What is the function of DHCP in a router’s LAN settings?
options: Distributes local IP addresses automatically to devices on the network
Authenticates the router to the ISP
Disables firewall settings for faster speeds

questions.questionText: What should a technician do if the router does not connect after entering correct PPPoE credentials?
options: Switch to DHCP mode and retry
Check cabling and reboot both the router and POE device
Disable the firewall to allow login
